nodes.name: coarse-graining
nodes.blurb: Any operation that throws away short-scale degrees of freedom while preserving long-scale ones. Each MLP layer is, we claim, an instance.

nodes.name: cortical down-state
nodes.blurb: A brief, profound silence of neuronal firing — the negative half of the cortical slow oscillation. In humans it appears, isolated, as the K-complex.

nodes.name: dendritic current
nodes.blurb: The transmembrane current along the dendritic tree that is the dominant source of extracellular field potentials.

nodes.name: distributed source modelling
nodes.blurb: An inverse-problem method that places thousands of dipoles on the cortical surface and lets the data choose which fire.

nodes.name: down-state
nodes.blurb: See cortical down-state. The label preferred when describing the slow oscillation in animal preparations.

nodes.name: EEG/MEG asynchrony
nodes.blurb: The disagreement between EEG and MEG over whether a spindle is synchronous — informative, not noise.

nodes.name: focal asynchronous oscillation
nodes.blurb: A spindle observed in MEG but not yet in EEG — a single cortical patch oscillating before global recruitment.

nodes.name: information-bottleneck
nodes.blurb: A formalism that describes the compression-relevance trade-off across layers of a deep network.

nodes.name: multiunit firing
nodes.blurb: The pooled spiking activity of many neurons recorded at a single electrode. Goes to zero during a down-state.

nodes.name: non-resistive extracellular medium
nodes.blurb: A medium whose impedance varies with frequency. Implies most forward models are systematically biased.

nodes.name: purely resistive
nodes.blurb: An extracellular medium whose impedance is the same at every frequency. The standard simplifying assumption — and, we argue, wrong.

nodes.name: recruitment time
nodes.blurb: The ~200 ms latency between a focal MEG spindle and its global EEG counterpart — the time the cortex takes to ignite.

nodes.name: renormalisation-group
nodes.blurb: A flow through theory space that integrates out short-distance physics to reveal effective long-distance behaviour. The mathematical heart of the depth-as-coarse-graining proposal.

nodes.name: RG flow
nodes.blurb: The trajectory of an effective theory under successive coarse-grainings.

nodes.name: spatially-propagating oscillator
nodes.blurb: A model in which spindle frequency is set by local cortical properties that vary in space, not by a single thalamic clock.

nodes.name: synchronous wave
nodes.blurb: The global, EEG-visible appearance of a spindle once enough cortical territory has been recruited.

nodes.name: thalamocortical core
nodes.blurb: The focally-projecting thalamocortical system that writes specific content into a single cortical patch.

nodes.name: thalamocortical matrix
nodes.blurb: The diffusely-projecting thalamocortical system that synchronises broad cortical territory.

nodes.name: travelling frequency front
nodes.blurb: A spindle whose central frequency drifts spatially across the cortex over its lifetime.
